Pembrolizumab for untreated metastatic non-small-cell lung cancer

Published:
15 January 2026
Last updated:
15 January 2026

1 Recommendations

1.1 Pembrolizumab is recommended, within its marketing authorisation, as an option for untreated metastatic non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC) in adults whose tumours express PD‑L1, only if the company provides it according to the commercial arrangement.

Why the committee made these recommendations

The clinical evidence comes from a single trial. Overall survival data were immature at the latest data cut, so the long-term survival benefit is uncertain. The comparator used in the economic model did not fully reflect NHS clinical practice. No health-related quality of life data were collected directly in the trial. Because of these issues the cost-effectiveness estimate (the ICER) was highly uncertain, but the most plausible ICER was within the range NICE considers an acceptable use of NHS resources, so pembrolizumab is recommended.

2 Information about pembrolizumab

Marketing authorisation: pembrolizumab is indicated for the first-line treatment of metastatic NSCLC in adults.
